fields.definition_origin: Edward L. Deci and Richard M. Ryan (University of Rochester) — formal articulation in the 1980s, foundational papers Deci 1971 and 1975, codified in 'Intrinsic Motivation and Self-Determination in Human Behavior' (1985, Plenum). Major synthesis: Ryan & Deci (2000) 'Self-Determination Theory and the Facilitation of Intrinsic Motivation, Social Development, and Well-Being' (American Psychologist, 55:68-78). Applied to UX by Nielsen Norman Group (Tanner Kohler, January 2022, 'Autonomy, Relatedness, and Competence in UX Design'). Yu-kai Chou and Springer 2024 gamification meta-analysis extend to product/onboarding design.
fields.core_mechanism: Three innate, universal psychological needs whose satisfaction is the substrate of intrinsic motivation: AUTONOMY (volition — actions feel self-chosen, not coerced), COMPETENCE (mastery — feeling capable of producing desired outcomes), RELATEDNESS (belonging — feeling cared-for and connected). In onboarding: AUTONOMY is satisfied by giving real choices (goal level, notification preferences, skip affordances) and avoiding forced single-paths; COMPETENCE is satisfied by early wins (a 30-second first action that succeeds, the 'aha' confirmation that the user 'got it'); RELATEDNESS is satisfied by warm microcopy, social proof ('5M users like you'), and parasocial coach voice (Headspace's Andy, Cal AI's plan-as-companion). SDT is the ETHICAL SCAFFOLDING for the Hook Model — without need-satisfaction, Hook loops produce shallow extrinsic engagement that decays; with need-satisfaction, they produce sustained intrinsic engagement. The strongest theoretical separator between ethical engagement and manipulation: SDT's controlled-vs-autonomous distinction (Ryan & Connell 1989).
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Intrinsic motivation correlates with striatal/ventral-tegmental dopaminergic activity (Murayama et al. 2010 PNAS — fMRI showed extrinsic reward undermines intrinsic motivation circuits). Autonomy support associates with reduced amygdala threat response and increased medial PFC self-referential processing. Relatedness engages oxytocin/social-bonding circuits and the temporoparietal junction. Competence feedback engages ventral striatum reward signaling.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Ryan & Deci (2000) American Psychologist — foundational synthesis, 10,000+ citations. (2) Wang et al. (2024) SDT meta-analysis on educational outcomes (selfdeterminationtheory.org) — confirmed need-support predicts academic outcomes, well-being, persistence; pooled effect sizes moderate-to-large. (3) Springer 2024 'Advancing Gamification Research and Practice with Three Underexplored Ideas in SDT' (Sailer & Sailer, TechTrends) — explicit SDT-aligned gamification framework. (4) Wiley/Springer meta-analyses on gamification: pooled g ≈ 0.654; intrinsic motivation g ≈ 0.638; autonomy/relatedness gains larger than competence gains. (5) Nalipay, King & Cai (2020) cross-cultural — autonomy predicted well-being equally well in 12-country East+West sample, refuting 'autonomy = Western individualism' critique. (6) Chirkov, Ryan, Kim & Kaplan (2003) — differentiated autonomy (volition, universal) from individualism (independence, culture-specific). (7) ACM TOCHI 2024 'SDT and HCI Games Research: Unfulfilled Promises and Unquestioned Paradigms' — flags measurement issues but confirms framework's HCI applicability. (8) Industry data: Duolingo's autonomy-supporting daily-XP picker is canonical; goal-customization users retain ~25% longer than default-only users (Duolingo blog data). (9) Onboarding-specific: Mau Baron Prayer Lock — explicit autonomy support ('Skip anytime', 'Choose your tradition') in long questionnaire is what prevents the sunk-cost flow from feeling coercive; conversion held while length increased.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Intrinsic Motivation Inventory (IMI) score, Basic Psychological Need Satisfaction Scale (BPNS), self-reported autonomy/competence/relatedness Likert items, D7/D30 retention, voluntary use frequency (vs. push-driven sessions), unsubscribe and uninstall rates, qualitative 'why I keep using this' interview themes, NPS, paid-conversion among non-FOMO-pressured cohorts.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Wiley/Springer SDT-gamification meta: intrinsic motivation g ≈ 0.638, retention/engagement g ≈ 0.654 (large effect by Cohen conventions). Duolingo autonomy-customized cohort retains ~25% longer than default-only cohort. NN/g 2022 case studies show explicit autonomy affordances reduce uninstall by 10-15% across surveyed apps. Cross-platform: granular notification opt-in (vs blanket all-on default) reduces D30 unsubscribe rate by 20-30% in industry telemetry.
fields.engineering_tactics: AUTONOMY: (1) every onboarding question must include a 'Skip' or 'Prefer not to say' option (kills coercion feeling); (2) defaults favor user well-being (notifications start opt-in granular, not all-on); (3) goal-setting screen must offer multiple commitment levels (Casual/Regular/Serious/Insane) with default at moderate, easy-change-anytime affordance, not pressure language ('Choose your commitment'); (4) post-paywall: 'Maybe later' must be visible, not hidden in a tiny x. COMPETENCE: (1) early-win design — the first action post-onboarding succeeds in <30 seconds (Cal AI's first meal log, Duolingo's 5-question lesson, Headspace's 1-minute breath); (2) immediate sensoryFeedback on every primary tap (.success on completion, .selection on choice); (3) visible mastery progress tied to actual skill, not vanity metrics; (4) just-in-time tutorials over upfront wizards. RELATEDNESS: (1) warm microcopy with personality (Mailchimp Voice & Tone canonical reference); (2) social proof punctuation cards mid-flow ('5M users like you said the same'); (3) parasocial coach voice (named character like Duo/Andy/Cal); (4) co-presence affordances (Strava kudos, Duolingo friend quests) opt-in by default, no public ranking unless explicitly chosen.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Duolingo daily-XP picker — autonomy via 4 commitment levels (Casual 5XP / Regular 10XP / Serious 20XP / Insane 50XP), default Regular, change-anytime in settings; competence via XP/skill tree visible mastery; relatedness via friend quests + Duo mascot voice. (2) Headspace onboarding — autonomy via session-length picker (1-30 min), competence via streak + completed sessions count, relatedness via Andy's voice as parasocial relatedness object. (3) Apple Watch onboarding — autonomy via customizable goals (Move/Exercise/Stand independently set), competence via ring-close progression, relatedness via Activity Sharing 'kudos'. (4) Mau Baron Prayer Lock — explicit autonomy ('Skip anytime', 'Choose your tradition'), competence (first prayer is short and successful), relatedness (community testimonials in onboarding). (5) Notion onboarding — high autonomy (configurable workspace from blank or template), competence (templates teach mastery), relatedness (collaborative comments). (6) Cal AI — autonomy via skip-able preference questions, competence via instant first-meal-log success, relatedness via plan-as-companion language ('Your Cal Plan'). ANTI-EXAMPLES: (a) Justanswer.com forcing chat-only interaction (NN/g cite); (b) Apps with 'Don't lose your streak!' loss-framed notifications (Duolingo's 2023 'Don't let Duo down' viral backlash); (c) FileZilla's onboarding-less competence-thwarting; (d) Default-on public leaderboards (relatedness-via-shame).
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile gives stronger relatedness signals via lock-screen widgets, push notifications (used carefully), live activities (iOS 16.1+), Android Live Updates, and haptic feedback that simulates social warmth. Mobile autonomy is more constrained by OS permission gates (push, ATT, location) — these become autonomy moments where the user feels in control; Contextless Permissions antipattern violates autonomy hard. Web has weaker relatedness channels but stronger autonomy via keyboard-first power-user flows (Linear, Raycast, Vercel command palettes). Both require the same SDT discipline; mobile just has more rope to hang yourself with (notification abuse).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Apple HIG explicitly emphasizes user control (autonomy) and 'people-first design'. Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) leans into expressive feedback (competence). Material 3 Expressive (I/O 2025, 18k-participant study) included engagement and well-being among measured outcomes — its expressive feedback boosts perceived competence by accelerating recognition speed up to 4x. SDT alignment is now ship-faster guidance, especially given regulatory pressure from EU Digital Fairness Act / UK AADC / California AADC.
fields.category_fit: Universal — every category benefits. Critical for: wellness/habit (engagement-extracting risk highest), edtech (SDT meta-analysis is built on education samples), AI-wrapper (parasocial relatedness is the differentiator), kids/family apps (regulatory exposure makes need-thwarting illegal). Strongest archetype fit: Caregiver, Sage, Magician. Less critical: pure-utility apps (calculator, flashlight) — needs aren't really engaged. DO NOT use need-thwarting tactics (loss-framed streaks, FOMO timers, default-on public profiles) in any consumer category in 2026.
fields.tooling_options: PostHog (track autonomy-supportive vs controlled-extrinsic copy A/B), OneSignal (granular notification preferences), Plotline (onboarding skip + customize affordances), Adapty (paywall with visible 'Maybe later'), RevenueCat (no-toggle compliant paywall). Microcopy auditing: Mailchimp Voice & Tone reference; CHT (Center for Humane Technology) audits.
fields.ai_dependency: none: SDT discipline is design + microcopy + default-setting; no LLM call required. Optional: small-model classifier in CI to flag need-thwarting copy ('Don\'t lose your streak', 'You\'re falling behind') and suggest autonomy-respecting rewrites ('Ready when you are', 'A 5-minute session is waiting').
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to design need-supporting, not need-thwarting, mechanics. Example fragment 1: 'For the daily-goal picker, support AUTONOMY: 4 preset levels (Casual 5min / Regular 15min / Serious 30min / Insane 60min) plus 5th Custom option with slider, default to Regular, easy-change-anytime affordance visible in onboarding (\'You can change this anytime\'). Support COMPETENCE: visible progress bar + sensoryFeedback .success on goal complete, milestone celebration at 7/30/100 days. Support RELATEDNESS: optional friend-quest invite OFF by default, no public leaderboard unless explicitly opted-in.' Example fragment 2: 'Audit every onboarding screen and notification for need-thwarting copy. Forbid loss-framed messaging (\'Don\'t lose your streak!\', \'You\'re falling behind\'). Replace with autonomy-respecting language (\'Ready when you are\', \'A 5-minute session is waiting\'). Output a copy-audit comment block listing each microcopy line and its autonomy/competence/relatedness mapping.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch needs explicit SDT cues because it produces manipulative gamification by default. Example fragment 1: 'Design an onboarding goal-setting screen with explicit AUTONOMY support: 4 preset cards (Casual 5min, Regular 15min, Serious 30min, Insane 60min) with a 5th Custom option. Default selection on Regular. Skip CTA visible at top-right. Microcopy below cards: \'You can change this anytime.\' Avoid pressure language (\'Choose your commitment\', \'Are you serious about your goals?\').' Example fragment 2: 'Friends/social tab with RELATEDNESS without coercion: small list of friends with kudos buttons (heart icon, brand color), no public leaderboard, no ranking number, no Compare-to-friends widget. Header copy: \'People cheering you on\'. Forbid red urgency badges and \'Friends are ahead of you\' messaging.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen reaches for the most engagement-extracting gamification by default — slot-machine variable rewards, FOMO countdown timers, public leaderboards, streak-loss notifications, default-on social pressure. This is need-thwarting and increasingly EU Digital Fairness Act / UK AADC risk. Counter-prompt: 'Apply Self-Determination Theory ethically. Forbid: streak-loss guilt notifications, default-on public leaderboards, FOMO countdown timers, slot-machine variable rewards. Require: opt-in for every social feature, granular notification controls, autonomy-respecting microcopy (no \'Don\'t lose your...\', no \'You\'re falling behind\'), and a competence-supporting (not vanity) progress display. Add a code comment block listing how each UI element supports autonomy/competence/relatedness or flag it for removal.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Streak-loss notifications that frame loss as moral failure — flips competence-support into controlled extrinsic motivation. (2) Default-on public leaderboards — thwarts autonomy and creates relatedness-via-shame. (3) Forced social signup (Login with Friends required) — autonomy violation. (4) Slot-machine variable rewards on top of existing competence systems — undermines intrinsic motivation per Murayama et al. 2010. (5) Generic robotic microcopy ('Action successful') — relatedness opportunity wasted. (6) Overwhelming first-session settings — competence-thwarting. (7) Manipulative re-engagement notifications timed to peak guilt windows. (8) Onboarding that hides skip affordances — autonomy violation that compounds with sunk-cost into resentment.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. SDT itself is benign — its ethical scaffolding is what reduces regulatory exposure of other tactics (gamification, streaks, notifications). Risk arises when products invoke the language of SDT while implementing need-thwarting mechanics — e.g. claiming 'we support your autonomy' while defaulting all notifications on. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft), DSA dark-pattern rules, UK AADC, California AADC all explicitly target need-thwarting design (default-on engagement loops, loss-framed nudges, default public profiles for minors). BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) is the precedent. Disney $10M FTC settlement (Dec 2025) sets enforcement tone for kids-app COPPA. Mitigate by designing default settings that favor user well-being and granular opt-outs.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization in onboarding (autonomy-aligned). Apple Guideline 4.8 sign-in alternatives (autonomy in auth). Apple Guideline 3.1.2 effective 2026-01 — toggle paywalls banned (autonomy-violating); replace with side-by-side plan selection. Google Play Data Safety requires declaration of behavioral profiling (relatedness-extraction surface). Apple HIG 'people-first' guidance is increasingly ship-faster reviewed.
fields.accessibility_implications: SDT and accessibility are deeply aligned: autonomy = choice = customizable text size, motion, audio, color modes. Required: prefers-reduced-motion respect (autonomy over experience), dynamic-type to 200%, VoiceOver/TalkBack support on every interactive element, granular notification controls (autonomy), sufficient contrast (WCAG 2.2 §1.4.3 4.5:1), target size ≥44×44pt iOS / 48dp Android (also ≥24×24px per WCAG 2.2 §2.5.8). Sound-on celebrations must respect mute. Skip and back affordances on every onboarding screen.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) SDT can be cargo-culted — products advertise 'autonomy-supportive' while shipping coercive defaults. (2) Need-satisfaction is contextual — daily reminders support autonomy for one user, thwart it for another. (3) Over-customization paradoxically reduces competence (Hick's Law collision) — autonomy needs sensible defaults. (4) Cross-cultural calibration on autonomy-as-individualism vs autonomy-as-volition (Chirkov et al. 2003 distinguished these). (5) BPNS and IMI scales have known cross-cultural psychometric issues; use multi-scale + qualitative triangulation. (6) ACM TOCHI 2024 critique: SDT in HCI is sometimes invoked without rigorous operationalization — measure, don't assume. (7) Tension with sunk-cost long-onboarding (item 4): ensure long flows don't sacrifice autonomy with hidden skip affordances.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Strongly validated cross-culturally. Nalipay, King & Cai (2020, 12-country sample) — autonomy predicted well-being equally well East and West, refuting earlier critique. Chirkov, Ryan, Kim & Kaplan (2003) — autonomy (volition) is universal, individualism (independence) is culture-specific. Practical calibration: relatedness register varies (US warmer; JP/KR more reserved; LATAM more familial; DACH more competence-focused); competence display varies (public ranking accepted in CN, EU prefers private progress). RTL languages need mirrored progress indicators. Confirmed valid; tactical execution must localize register, not principle.
fields.prescribed_action: Wire onboarding for need-satisfaction: every screen must offer a real choice (autonomy: skip or 'Prefer not to say'), every action must produce immediate sensory + visual confirmation (competence: sensoryFeedback .success on completion), and every microcopy line must be warm, human, and free of loss-framed coercion (relatedness: no 'Don't lose your streak'). Audit defaults — they must favor user well-being (granular notifications opt-in, no public leaderboard by default, 'Maybe later' visible on paywall).
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Every questionnaire question must include autonomy affordances: a Skip / 'Prefer not to say' option, easy back-edit, no required-field UX traps. Sequencing should produce competence early (low-friction first question that succeeds — preference/identity ahead of demographic). Microcopy should produce relatedness ('Glad you're here', 'You're not alone in this') without becoming saccharine. SDT is the ETHICAL governor on the sunk-cost questionnaire pattern (item 4) — together they prevent length from converting to coercion.
fields.aha_relevance: SDT is what makes Aha land as joy rather than as relief-from-pressure. A user whose autonomy was respected during the questionnaire experiences the AI plan reveal as competence-affirming ('I helped build this'). A user whose autonomy was violated experiences it as manipulation ('They strung me along to sell me'). Need-satisfied Aha produces D30+ retention; need-thwarted Aha produces uninstall after the trial.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Universal — SDT applies to every archetype. Strongest fit: Caregiver (autonomy + relatedness via gentle voice), Sage (autonomy + competence via clear knowledge), Magician (competence via mastery + relatedness via parasocial guide). Conflicts when archetype voice produces controlling copy: aggressive Hero ('Crush your goals!') can violate autonomy; aggressive Lover ('Don't be alone') can violate autonomy. Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json. Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Self_Determination_Theory.json (deeper foundational treatment).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Manipulative streak/loss-aversion (need-thwarting); Forced social signup (autonomy violation); Default-on public leaderboards (relatedness-via-shame); Toggle paywall (autonomy violation, banned 2026-01); Premature Authentication (competence violation — user gated before they feel capable).
sources: [Ryan & Deci (2000) — SDT and the Facilitation of Intrinsic Motivation (PDF)](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/SDT/documents/2000_RyanDeci_SDT.pdf)
[Autonomy, Relatedness, and Competence in UX Design — Nielsen Norman Group (Tanner Kohler, 2022)](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/autonomy-relatedness-competence/)
[Wang et al. (2024) — SDT meta-analysis on educational outcomes](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/2024_WangWangEtAl_MetaEdu.pdf)
[Nalipay, King & Cai (2020) — Autonomy is equally important across East and West](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0140197119302295)
[ACM TOCHI 2024 — SDT and HCI Games Research: Unfulfilled Promises](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3673230)
